intro: 108 AI items this week (71 India-relevant) across 10 sources.

items.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
items.summary: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

items.title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost no…

items.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
items.summary: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

items.title: Create, edit and star in videos with two Google Vids updates
items.summary: Gemini Omni and personal avatars in Google Vids make video creation easier than ever.

items.title: Why AMI Labs’ Alexandre LeBrun won’t call his AI ‘AGI’ or ‘superintelligence’
items.summary: While everyone in AI is chasing "superintelligence," Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun’s world model startup, AMI Labs, dismisses the word.

items.title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
items.summary: The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion.

items.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
items.summary: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

items.title: Fine-tune video and image models at scale with NVIDIA NeMo Automodel and 🤗 Diffusers
Introducing Real World VoiceEQ: Measuring the human quality of voice AI
Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
items.summary: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

items.title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identit…

items.title: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
items.summary: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fu…

items.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
items.summary: DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward software designed for AI agents instead of just humans.

items.title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
items.summary: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality:…

items.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
items.summary: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

items.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
items.summary: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

items.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
items.summary: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

items.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
items.summary: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI…

items.title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
items.summary: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to actively block unauthorized AI training.

items.title: Apple’s lawsuit couldn’t come at a worse time for OpenAI
items.summary: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI…

items.title: Pocket-size AI: Powerful phones star at China show
items.summary: Tech firms are developing AI agent smartphones that could revolutionize mobile use. These advanced phones aim to perform tasks using voice commands and AI. Major app companies are restricting access, which hinders the AI agent's functionality. Companies like Nubia, Honor, and …

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 access plans change from July 20: Here’s what’s new
Liftoff for Skyroot’s Vikram-1; Building AI voice infra for Indian languages
items.summary: Vikram-1 is a four-stage launch vehicle designed to carry payloads of up to 350 kg to low Earth orbit (LEO), the region of space where many Earth observation, communication and scientific satellites operate.

items.title: Education, AI, health: test your business creativity with Edition 241 of our weekly quiz!
items.summary: This insightful feature from YourStory tests and strengthens your business acumen! Here are 5 questions to kick off this 241st quiz. Ready?

items.title: Researchers build AI camera that reconstructs particle paths using handful of photons
77% of Indian professionals use AI at work; AI-skilled workers earn more: Naukri report
US data center protests go national as backlash grows
items.summary: Opponents of AI data center expansion plan nationwide protests this Saturday. HumansFirst is coordinating demonstrations in at least 125 locations across the United States. Protesters voice concerns about environmental impact and community autonomy. A recent poll indicated low…

items.title: Google AI Mode adds more Connected Apps, including YouTube Music

careers.note: Demand snapshot for 2026. ML Engineer is the most in-demand AI title; agentic-AI and deep-learning skills carry the highest pay premiums; Python + one cloud is the baseline. Specialisation beats generalisation for freshers.

paths.note: #1 AI title — build, deploy & maintain models in production. Best all-round entry into AI.
RAG, tool-use & multi-agent systems. ~40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by year-end.
Neural nets for vision & speech. Highest premiums; usually needs strong maths / grad study.
Ship & scale models on the cloud. Cloud AI certs add 20–25% to salary.
Foundational, widest entry door; a reliable first step into AI.
Imaging, robotics, autonomous systems, medical AI.
Powers chatbots, search & agents — the hottest application area.
5–8 yrs experience; $140k–$332k bands. Translates AI into business value.
Alignment, evals, red-teaming & policy — newer but expanding fast.

fresher.why: The lingua franca of ML — non-negotiable.
Linear algebra, probability & calculus — enough to reason about models.
Regression, classification, evaluation, the ML workflow.
Build & train neural nets; PyTorch dominates research & jobs.
Ship a small LLM app — the most visible skill to recruiters now.
Real AI work is mostly data; SQL is everywhere.
AWS leads (~40% of postings), then Azure & GCP. Version everything.
A deployed project that solves a real problem is the #1 differentiator.

experienced.why: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, model/versioning — production is where value lives.
Multi-agent orchestration, tool-use, evals — the surging frontier.
AWS/Azure/GCP architecture; certs add a 20–25% salary premium.
Adapt, measure and harden models for a domain.
Cost & latency engineering for large models.
Trade-offs across data, latency, cost & reliability at scale.
CV, NLP, recsys, healthcare — depth beats breadth at senior levels.
Leading teams and tying models to measurable outcomes.

profile.name: Harman Singh Sandhu
profile.blurb: I build AI/ML projects and write about them. This site is one of them — an automated daily AI digest.

repos.name: Ace-rentals
repos.desc: Ace Rentals: A full-stack property rental platform built with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB. Features user authentication, image uploads, geocoding, reviews, and secure account management. Inspired by Airbnb.

repos.name: ai-weekly-report
repos.desc: My personalized AI newsletter that runs on an agentic AI
